text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1 'Edge of Anarchy', A. Old Fishery — p.17.

A decrepit but solid two-floor fishery on pilings over the Jeggare River, on a 13-ft embankment (Athletics DC 15 to climb the slick bank). Doors are wood (Hardness 5, low HP; Thievery DC 15 to pick, the brass key bypasses). Rendering rancid fish into slurry with orphan labor is the cover business.

Standing hazards: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.6y55S5DpXqnF40WL]{Slippery Boardwalk} (A3) and @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.xMhIQZS6GokTsulZ]{Rotten Ship Deck} (A9) — both can dump a PC into the water with @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.lsdWSvGJ81hDy4it]{the jigsaw shark} (A12).

Day/night: see the Run Sheet. Map: use Racooze's CotCT Battlemaps (Old Fishery) — none shipped (copyright). Scene-planning lives in the project scenes pack.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1 'Edge of Anarchy', A1 — p.18.

Locked double doors (Thievery DC 15; knocking or a failed/loud pick brings @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.7uhbgkK2IOZOlJb3]{Yargin} from A6). A drooping signpost; brine-and-dead-fish reek.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1 'Edge of Anarchy', A2 — p.18.

A 15-ft dock with tar-caked slurry barrels; stairs down to a riverside door near the water. Doors to A7 are open by day; the door to A8 is always locked (Thievery DC 15).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1 'Edge of Anarchy', A3 — p.18–20.

The @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.6y55S5DpXqnF40WL]{Slippery Boardwalk} clings to the south wall 13 ft above the river. Moving more than a Step (or fighting) on it risks a fall (Reflex/Acrobatics DC 15; crit fail → into the shark water). Heavy loads or two adjacent creatures can collapse a stretch. The A6 door is locked (Thievery DC 15).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1 'Edge of Anarchy', A4 — p.19.

A disused customer room; under the desk sleeps @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.KHTYbQgR5hnFZdGL]{Bloo}, Yargin's foul cur (use Guard Dog, rename the token). He attacks strangers on sight and his barks rouse the fishery. E1, Trivial (20 XP). Silence him fast (Stealth/Diplomacy to avoid, or a quick kill).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1 'Edge of Anarchy', A5 — p.20.

The three thugs' bunkroom. They distrust each other and keep no valuables here.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1 'Edge of Anarchy', A6 — p.19–21.

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.7uhbgkK2IOZOlJb3]{Yargin Balko}, Gaedren's acid-throwing fence, works here. E2, Low (40 XP). He opens with thrown Acid Flasks, then a light crossbow; if losing he flees down the hidden trapdoor to warn Gaedren.

Hidden trapdoor (Perception DC 18 to spot; barred — Athletics DC 17 to force) drops to A13 — a flanking shortcut onto the boss. Yargin carries the brass key (all fishery doors).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1 'Edge of Anarchy', A7 — p.20–22.

Slurry-prep room; 4 orphans toil under @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.qH96VshE6CZqfajv]{Hookshanks Gruller}, a gnome who hides among the children (Perception DC 17 to pick him out). E3, Low (40 XP). He can surrender and inform. The 4 @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.UQr3ApF78GW7eghw]{orphans} are non-combatants — rally them (Diplomacy DC 15). Slippery floor (Acrobatics DC 15 if moving fast). Petty-cash cabinet locked (Thievery DC 15).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1 'Edge of Anarchy', A8 — p.21–22.

The main slurry floor (8-ft tarred vat, catwalks, a hole to the river). @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.mHUhxeaalWkTU1h4]{Giggles} oversees 5 orphans by day (26 sleep here at night). E4, Low (40 XP; 60 with Bloo at night). Giggles tries to capture PCs nonlethally for Gaedren, then fights to the death (Orc Ferocity). The hole in the floor drops to the underpier/water — beware feeding PCs to @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.lsdWSvGJ81hDy4it]{the shark}.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1 'Edge of Anarchy', A9 — p.23–24.

A derelict ship lashed to the pilings. The @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.xMhIQZS6GokTsulZ]{Rotten Ship Deck} (foredeck) collapses under Medium+ weight (Reflex DC 16 or fall to A11 for 1d6 + prone among the spiders); the stern is sound. Stairs down to the spider nest (A10/A11).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1 'Edge of Anarchy', A10 — p.24.

Aft cabin lair of a single @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.1M94tm3wnSRGcTG5]{drain spider} that lunges at the first to enter. E5, Trivial (20 XP). Repelled by the vermin repellent looted in A13.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1 'Edge of Anarchy', A11 — p.24.

Four more @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.1M94tm3wnSRGcTG5]{drain spiders} infest the hold. E6, Moderate (80 XP) — or trivialized if the PCs coat themselves in vermin repellent first. A hull secret door (Perception DC 17; +2 if Survival DC 15 tracked Gaedren's path) leads to A12 and the boss.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1 'Edge of Anarchy', A12 — p.24–25.

A floating walkway under the fishery; Gaedren's skiffs (his escape route) are tied here. @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.lsdWSvGJ81hDy4it]{A jigsaw shark} patrols the water. E7, Low (40 XP), avoidable — it attacks anyone in the water but only leaps onto the walkway if attacked first. The small (2½-ft) locked door (Thievery DC 17) leads to A13.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1 'Edge of Anarchy', A13 — p.24–26.

The climax. A chamber over a water pit holding @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.2rMLYkUR47ZCQMUg]{Gobblegut} (use Crocodile, rename the token); two 5-ft walkways cross the pit. @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.RKfT6vJ5guinSBjo]{Gaedren Lamm} sorts loot here.

E8, SEVERE (120 XP) at level 1 (Moderate at level 2): Gaedren (L2) + Gobblegut (L2). Gaedren opens with Spur the Beast — shooting Gobblegut to enrage it — then snipes from across the pit while the gator menaces anyone near the water. He flees toward the A12 skiffs at ≤8 HP. Reaching him means crossing the gator's water. Full tactics & party-size scaling: Encounters journal.

Loot on the tables: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.ymuRwMg6WfWIwkne]{a darkwood coffer + 20 shiver doses + 7 vermin repellent}.

⚠ Track: if the PCs leave Gaedren's body here, his son Rolth animates it as a zombie in the Dead Warrens (Ch.1 Part 3, area D13).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1 'Edge of Anarchy', A14 — p.26–28.

Gaedren's filthy bedroom/study. A locked strongbox (rusty iron key on Gaedren; or Thievery DC 15) holds his hoard, including @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.iNQaVcbkSM0VPXsa]{Queen Ileosa's Brooch} (launches the next beat). On the dresser, a hatbox holds Zellara's severed head (Medicine DC 14: dead for weeks — the reveal) and @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.DPDen8gco6rznX9Y]{her haunted harrow deck}. Atop the strongbox: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.I7HQNtjI9yPvQ4fA]{Gaedren's coded ledger} (Society DC 17 to decode). Full parcel: Treasure journal.
